summary: This April Fool's Day post pretends to abandon tech blogging in favor of cat content, introducing Jeff's two cats Floyd and Elsie. The joke included replacing the blog's front page with obnoxiously intrusive advertisements. Beyond the humor, the post genuinely discusses the joys of pet ownership and how cats interact with computer users. Jeff advocates for adopting rescue animals and notes that two cats are more entertaining than one. The meta-joke comments on both the banality of cat blogging and the horrors of early-2000s web advertising.
key_points: The post was an April Fool's joke combining cat blogging with intentionally terrible advertisements
Jeff adopted Floyd in 2001 as a shy rescue cat, then added Elsie a year later to keep Floyd company
Cats and computers create an inevitable conflict as cats love positioning themselves in front of keyboards
Two cats are better than one because they entertain each other and their owners
The joke works because cat blogging represents the antithesis of Coding Horror's usual technical content
money_quotes: If you're a cat owner and a computer user, you know how difficult it can be to get anything done with a cat around.
Cats love nothing more than curling up directly in front of your keyboard for attention.
We figured once you have the first cat, adding a second cat is hardly any additional work, and they can entertain each other when you're not around.
I'm not necessarily a 'cat person' so much as I am an animal lover.
I think the whole Dog vs. Cat war is ludicrous.
Cats are a far better match for lazy people like me.
key_insight: Even a technical blogger can't resist the universal experience of cats interrupting computer work, making cat content the perfect April Fool's subversion of a programming blog.
